target.name: GrayMatter Robotics
target.founder: Ariyan Kabir, Brual Shah, Sangam Pant

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $300M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $1.8B.

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: The Bot Company
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
GrayMatter Robotics is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company that has raised over $70M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of nine firms: one public entity and eight private companies in the same sector at Series B or Series C stages, including peers such as Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Hadrian, and Nimble Robotics.

Two triangulation methods are applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to a $300M last post-money valuation, applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $570M. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Bedrock ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Hadrian ($1.6B), and Nimble ($1.0B)—produces a median of $1.6B, with a range of $1.0B to $1.8B. These methods imply a wide valuation band: the stage-progression method suggests the lower bound, while peer multiples suggest substantially higher valuations for companies at comparable maturity.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $484M (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median of $1.1B. The substantial dispersion reflects key uncertainties: the extent to which GrayMatter's technology, market position, and growth trajectory align with higher-valued peers versus earlier-stage comparables, and whether the $300M base valuation reflects a pre-money or post-money figure. Resolution of these inputs and clearer benchmarking against the most relevant peer subset would narrow the range materially.
